findings.message: Self-hosted runner setup is a hard Week 1 dependency — GitHub-hosted runners are structurally insufficient for UE5.4 Shipping builds (disk: 14 GB free on Windows vs 100+ GB required; RAM: 16 GB vs 32-64 GB required). No v2 CI exists without this. Setup time: 2-4h one-time.

Screenshot-diff CI must be scoped to tagged release builds only (aero-v2-* tags). Running on every PR would exhaust the $20/month Google Maps Tiles API cap in days — estimated $1.60-$8.00 per CI session (400-2000 tile requests at 4 geo-poses). This is a CI infrastructure constraint, not a test strategy choice.

Linux Shipping build feasibility depends on operator hardware. If the Linux self-hosted runner cannot be registered and pass a compile-check by end of Week 1, Linux should be flagged at-risk immediately — not discovered as a slip at Week 3 per the current D17 wording.
OQ-13 resolved: tag naming convention locked as aero-v1-<semver> for Three.js releases and aero-v2-<semver> for UE5 releases. Workflow trigger filters use these prefixes for unambiguous separation.

OQ-08 resolved: recommended runner topology is self-hosted hybrid — v1 CI on ubuntu-latest (unchanged), v2 builds on self-hosted [windows/linux, ue5-builder]. Monthly cost: $5-36 (electricity + tile API).

OQ-05 partially resolved (DevOps side): .tile-cache/ gitignored layout, 5GB cap, LRU eviction by mtime, make tile-cache-populate Makefile target. Engine-side Cesium tile redirect implementation assigned to game-developer (see objection in report). OQ-03 (1Password item ID) requires operator action.

decisions_required: OQ-03: Operator must create the 1Password item 'aero-fighters-v2/google-maps-tiles-api-key' and record the item ID.
OQ-10: software-architect must propose the exact Cesium for Unreal plugin version/commit hash compatible with UE5.4 — devops will express it as a committed .cesium-version pin file.
D17 if-slip rule: synthesis SPEC should add a Week 1 runner-availability gate in addition to the Week 3 Cesium rendering gate (proposed wording in Objections section of report).
